app.title: mod player
app.homeTitle: Online Tracker Music Player - Play MOD, XM, S3M, IT Files Online
app.description: An online web player for tracker music (MOD, XM, S3M, IT and 68+ formats) with realtime audio visualizers, interactive channel control, and custom playlists.

settings.title: Settings
settings.engineDesc: Select which audio engine to use. Chiptune3 is recommended for best tracker format support.

engines.bassoonDesc: Only supports .xm / .mod. Falls back to Chiptune3 then FFmpeg for other formats.
engines.chiptune3Desc: Supports 20+ tracker formats (.xm/.mod/.it/.s3m/.mptm/.umx …). Falls back to FFmpeg for other formats.
engines.ffmpegDesc: Converts all audio formats to MP3. Broadest support, slower startup.

comments.title: Comments

intro.title: Guide
intro.introWelcome: Your gateway to enjoying tracker music in 68+ formats — from the legendary MOD and XM to modern MPTM and UMX — drawing from the vast collection at
intro.introWelcomeNote: Tracker Player is more than just a player — it's a full-featured tracker music platform with a gorgeous, responsive interface, real-time pattern visualization, per-channel oscilloscopes, and a suite of tools designed to make discovering and enjoying tracked music effortless on any device.
intro.introWhatBody1: Tracker Player is an open-source personal project dedicated to bringing the charm of tracker music into the modern browser. It is not a brand-new audio engine, but rather a deep integration and secondary development built upon existing excellent open-source projects, creating a complete user experience system.
intro.introWhatBody2Tail: , a browser-based Amiga MOD/XM tracker written in pure JavaScript by Steffest, faithfully recreating the classic tracker playback experience. Building on this foundation, the project gradually integrated Chiptune3 (a custom WebAssembly engine based on libopenmpt) and FFmpeg server-side transcoding, expanding format support from the original MOD/XM to over 68 formats.
intro.introWhatBody3: From playlist search and filtering, audio visualization, user account systems to internationalization support, Tracker Player has built a complete ecosystem of peripheral features around the player core, aiming to let users enjoy the unique charm of tracker music without any technical background.
intro.introTrackerBody1: Tracker music (also known as module music or MOD music) is a unique form of digital music that emerged in the late 1980s on the Commodore Amiga. Unlike MP3s or streaming audio, tracker files contain both the musical score (notes, patterns, effects) and the instrument samples — like a self-contained music project file. This makes tracker files remarkably compact while allowing incredible musical complexity.
intro.introTrackerBody2: The format was pioneered by Karsten Obarski's Ultimate Soundtracker in 1987, and rapidly evolved through the demoscene — a vibrant subculture where programmers, musicians, and artists compete to create stunning audiovisual works. Over the decades, dozens of tracker formats emerged, each pushing the boundaries of what was possible: more channels, better effects, synthesized instruments, VST plugin support, and even full mixing capabilities. Today, the tracker scene remains active worldwide, with new music being released daily on sites like The Mod Archive.
intro.introTrackerBody3: To this day, tracker music continues to thrive within the global demoscene community and among retro computing enthusiasts. Tracker Player hopes to introduce more people to this unique art form that carries the history of computer music, through a modern interface and convenient interaction.
intro.tutorialLead: This guide walks you through every feature of Tracker Player, from finding your first track to customizing the player for your workflow.
intro.tutorialStep1Body: The playlist is the heart of Tracker Player. Use the search bar at the top to find tracks by filename or title. Below the search bar, you'll find a powerful set of filters:
tutorialStep1Filters: Format Filter — Click to open a multi-select grid of all available formats. Use the preset buttons to quickly select groups: Bassoon (MOD/XM), Major Tracker (MOD/XM/IT/S3M/MPTM), Chiptune3 All (56 formats), or All Supported.
Channels Filter — Narrow down by channel count: 4 channels (classic MOD era), 8 channels, or 8+ channels (modern multitrack).
Size Filter — Filter by file size: small (<100KB), medium, or large (>1MB).
Tracker Name Filter — Type a tracker name (e.g., "Impulse Tracker", "FastTracker") to find music created with specific software.
Sort Options — Order the playlist by ID, title, filename, size, or modification date, in ascending or descending order.
Source Selector — Switch between All tracks, your Favorites, and your Play History.
intro.tutorialStep2Body: Click any track in the playlist to start playback. The player loads instantly — most files begin playing in under a second. While a track plays, you'll see:
tutorialStep2Details: Track Info — Title, artist, format, channel count, and track number displayed above the player controls.
Play/Pause & Navigation — Large play/pause button with playback mode selector: Shuffle (random), In Order (sequential), Loop One, or Stop After Current.
Next Track — Skip to the next track manually, or let your chosen playback mode handle it.
Volume & Progress — Adjust volume with the slider, seek through the track, and see elapsed/remaining time.
Like Button — Click the heart to add a track to your favorites (requires account).
Download — Save the original tracker file to your device.
ModArchive Link — Open the track's page on The Mod Archive for more context and community comments.
Track Info Modal — View detailed metadata: tracker name, date, duration, container type, subsongs, channel count, instruments, samples, orders, patterns, and any embedded message from the composer.
intro.tutorialStep3Body: Tracker Player brings the music to life with two complementary real-time visualizations:
tutorialStep3Details: Pattern View — See the actual tracker pattern data scrolling in real time, rendered in WebGL for silky-smooth performance. Each column represents a channel, each row is a note event. Notes, instruments, volume, and effect commands are all displayed — just like in a classic tracker editor. Click the pattern toggle to switch between vertical (traditional) and horizontal (widescreen) layouts.

Per-Channel Oscilloscopes — Below the player, you'll see a grid of waveform displays — one for each audio channel in the track. These are powered by our custom C-level waveform processor built into libopenmpt, which extracts individual channel audio before final mixing. Watch each channel's waveform scroll in real time, giving you a visual fingerprint of how the music is constructed — kick drum on channel 1, bass on channel 2, lead on channel 3, and so on.
Both visualizations can be toggled on/off in Settings for performance or preference.
intro.tutorialStep4Body: Tracker Player is highly customizable. Access settings through the gear icon (top bar) or the Settings ear (left sidebar):
tutorialStep4Settings: Playback Engine — Choose between BassoonTracker (classic MOD/XM), Chiptune3 (recommended, 20+ formats), or FFmpeg (broadest format support via server-side conversion).

Theme — Pick from 16 color themes. Light themes include Light, Retro (Solarized), Ocean (Teal), Forest (Green), Strawberry (Pink), Blue, Pink, Cyan, Orange, and Gray. Dark themes include Dark, Midnight (Deep Purple), Golden (Black & Gold), Purple, Red, and Green.
Language — 52 interface languages. Your preference is saved locally.
Volume — Set a default volume level and optionally force it on every track.
Loudness Normalization — Automatically adjust playback volume for consistent listening (on by default).
Max Loop Count — Tracker songs often loop forever. Set a maximum loop count to prevent endless playback (default: 1).
Display Toggles — Show or hide the scope visualization, pattern view, and introduction section.
intro.tutorialStep5Body: Create a free account to unlock additional features:
tutorialStep5Details: Favorites — Heart any track to save it to your favorites list, accessible from the playlist source selector.
Play History — Every track you play is automatically recorded. Browse your history to rediscover tracks.
Cross-Device Sync — Your favorites and history follow you across devices when you log in.
Privacy — Your account stores only favorites and history. No personal data is collected or shared.
intro.tutorialStep6Body: Every track in Tracker Player has a permanent, shareable URL. Copy the link from your browser's address bar while a track is playing — it will look like /song/123456/filename.mod. Send it to friends, post it on social media, or bookmark it for later. The link will always open the same track.
intro.tutorialStep6Tip: Pro tip: On mobile, add Tracker Player to your home screen for a native app-like experience. It works great as a Progressive Web App with offline caching.
intro.tutorialClosing: Happy listening — and enjoy the music!

tutorialMock.labelFilter: Source dropdown: All / Favorites / History  |  Search box: search by filename or title
tutorialMock.labelFormatFilter: Click “Format” to open the multi-select panel · Channel/Size dropdown filters · Multi-field sort
tutorialMock.annotPlaylist: Click any row to start playback · Each row shows: number · title · format · channels · size · filename

tutorialMock.annotAccount: Free registration · Only favorites and history are stored · No tracking, no ads

intro.changelogIntro: Tracker Player has evolved significantly from its origins as a simple MOD/XM player. Here's a timeline of major milestones:

items: Integrated libopenmpt as a third playback engine, supporting 68+ tracker formats with bit-accurate playback.
Developed custom waveform_processor in C — modifications to libopenmpt's source code enabling per-channel audio extraction before final mixing.
Compiled libopenmpt + waveform_processor to WebAssembly via Emscripten for in-browser execution.
Built WebGL-based pattern view with both vertical (traditional tracker) and horizontal (widescreen) display modes.
Added per-channel oscilloscope visualization — live scrolling waveforms for each audio channel, powered by the custom waveform processor.
Created adaptive font sizing and pixel-perfect text rendering in WebGL visualizer.
Implemented hard clipping protection to prevent uint8 texture overflow in waveform visualizers.
Added multi-channel signal normalization and noise gating for cleaner oscilloscope displays.
Fixed timing drift in Chiptune3 AudioWorklet for rock-solid playback synchronization.
Expanded format support to 56 formats in Chiptune3 + additional formats in libopenmpt.
Added user account system with registration, login, and favorites/history sync.
Added track info modal with comprehensive metadata display.
Added download modal — download original tracker files or convert to MP3.
Added horizontal layout mode for pattern display (transposed channel and row data).
Added theme-specific row highlight alpha variables for WebGL shader backgrounds.
Developed Chiptune3 — a custom WebAssembly audio engine supporting 20+ tracker formats with per-channel output.
Implemented AudioWorklet-based playback for low-latency, glitch-free audio.
Built dual-canvas oscilloscope system — spectrum analyzer (frequency domain) and waveform display (time domain).
Added WebGL rendering pipeline for high-performance visualizations.
Introduced 16 color themes with CSS custom properties and runtime switching.
Built the ear-bar sidebar (InfoDrawer) with 5 panels: Guide, Settings, Theme, Language, Account.
Added 52-language internationalization system with locale detection and fallback chains.
Implemented responsive mobile layout with safe-area support and collapsible navigation.
Added playlist search, format filter panel with preset buttons, and multi-field sort.
Built pagination controls with first/prev/-10/+10/next/last navigation.
Initial release as 'MOD Player' — a web-based player for MOD and XM tracker music.
Integrated BassoonTracker by Steffest as the core playback engine — browser-based Amiga tracker in JavaScript.
Built playlist interface with table view showing track number, title, and filename.
Added basic playback controls: play, pause, next, playback mode selection.
Created Settings panel with volume control, max loop setting, and display toggles.
Implemented local storage for user preferences (theme, language, settings).
Added introductory guide section explaining what tracker music is and how to use the player.
Sourced initial music library from CoolModFiles.com and The Mod Archive.
Designed the responsive layout foundation: header, player column, playlist column, footer.
Added dark and light theme support with CSS custom properties.

intro.creditsIntro: Tracker Player is built on the shoulders of an incredible open-source ecosystem. Our entire 135,400+ track library is sourced from The Mod Archive — the internet's definitive repository for tracker music. We're deeply grateful to every contributor whose work made this project possible.
intro.creditsBassoonDesc: Created by Steffest. A faithful browser-based reimplementation of the classic Amiga MOD and XM trackers — ProTracker and FastTracker II — written entirely in JavaScript. BassoonTracker provides the authentic, warm playback that started this project, with full support for classic 4-channel MODs and multi-channel XMs including envelopes, filters, and effects.
intro.creditsChiptune3Desc: Created by DrSnuggles — an open-source JavaScript library that wraps libopenmpt in an AudioWorklet for browser playback of 68+ tracker formats. For this project, we customized the AudioWorklet processor to extract per-channel waveform data (powering the multi-channel oscilloscopes), added automatic gain control, and strengthened race-condition handling for rapid track switching. Chiptune3 serves as the bridge between the browser's Web Audio API and the decoding power of our patched libopenmpt build, and is our recommended default engine.
intro.creditsLibopenmptDesc: Developed by OpenMPT contributors. The definitive open-source tracker playback library, supporting 68+ module formats with unmatched accuracy. We modified libopenmpt's C source code — adding a custom waveform_processor that captures per-channel PCM data inside the mixer before final output — and compiled it to WebAssembly via Emscripten. Our patched WASM build replaces the upstream pre-compiled binary bundled with Chiptune3, enabling the per-channel oscilloscopes and forming the decoding backbone for all 68+ formats.
intro.creditsNuxtDesc: The Vue.js meta-framework powering our entire frontend and backend. Nuxt provides server-side rendering for fast page loads and SEO, Nitro server for API routes, SQLite database integration, and automatic code-splitting for optimal performance.
intro.creditsVueDesc: The progressive JavaScript framework used for our component-based UI. Vue's Composition API, reactivity system, and single-file components made it possible to build and maintain a complex player interface with clean, modular code.
intro.creditsTailwindDesc: The utility-first CSS framework used for styling. Tailwind's design token system enables our 16-theme architecture — every color, shadow, and border adapts to the active theme through CSS custom properties generated from our Tailwind configuration.
intro.creditsEmscriptenDesc: The LLVM-based compiler toolchain that compiles C/C++ code to WebAssembly. We use Emscripten to compile our patched libopenmpt — including the custom waveform_processor — into a single-file WASM module that executes at near-native speed inside the browser's AudioWorklet.
intro.creditsAudioWorkletDesc: The W3C Web Audio API specification that enables low-latency, glitch-free audio processing on a dedicated real-time thread. The AudioWorklet is where our Chiptune3 engine runs the compiled libopenmpt WASM, independent of the main UI thread, ensuring smooth playback regardless of interface activity.
intro.creditsWebGLDesc: The browser graphics API that renders our real-time tracker pattern view. Pattern note data is streamed to a WebGL canvas at 60 fps, faithfully reproducing the scrolling-note look of classic tracker editors like ProTracker and FastTracker II.
intro.creditsModArchiveDesc: The internet's premier repository for tracked music, hosting over 150,000 modules from thousands of artists across four decades. The Mod Archive is the sole source of the music in our library and an irreplaceable institution for the tracking community.
introFeatures: A library of 135,400+ tracker music files spanning 68+ formats, from 4-channel MODs to 256-channel epics.
Triple-engine architecture: BassoonTracker (classic JS), Chiptune3 (AudioWorklet + libopenmpt WASM), and FFmpeg (server-side transcoding) — automatically selected for each file.
Real-time tracker pattern view rendered in WebGL — watch the notes scroll by just like in a classic tracker editor.
Per-channel waveform oscilloscopes — see each channel's audio output as a live scrolling waveform, powered by custom C-level waveform extraction.
Responsive design that adapts beautifully to any screen: desktop, tablet, or phone — including horizontal pattern mode for widescreen.
16 meticulously crafted color themes — from crisp light to deep midnight, solarized retro, warm golden, and everything in between.
52 interface languages — making tracker music accessible to listeners worldwide.
Powerful search, multi-select format filter with preset buttons (Bassoon, Major Tracker, Chiptune3, All Supported), channel count filter, and multi-field sort.
Account system with favorites and play history — sync your library across all your devices.
Permanent shareable song links — every track gets a unique URL like /song/123456/filename.mod.
Download original tracker files or convert to MP3 with a single click.
Track info panel showing metadata: title, artist, tracker, format, channels, instruments, samples, orders, patterns, and embedded message.
Completely free, open-source, and community-driven — no ads, no tracking, no paywalls.
